chunk_text: After Wayfair v. Remitcorp, state CA treats remote sellers as having economic nexus when sales into the state exceed $500,000 or 200 transactions in a calendar year. Synthetic seller SYN-001 shipped catalog SKUs from out-of-state warehouses; nexus review must compare trailing twelve-month gross receipts against the statutory floor before registering for sales and use tax.

Payroll nexus for state NY attaches when a remote employer has employees performing services in-state on more than 14 days, or when wage withholding for SYN-002 exceeds $40,000 in a quarter. Remote-work policies that place a single W-2 employee in NY can trigger income-tax withholding registration even without a physical office.

Property tax nexus in state TX follows ownership or control of business personal property valued above $50,000. Synthetic tenant SYN-003 staged leased POS terminals and spare inventory at a third-party logistics site; assessors look to situs of tangible assets rather than corporate domicile when asserting ad valorem liability.

After Wayfair v. Remitcorp, state WA treats remote sellers as having economic nexus when sales into the state exceed $100,000 or 200 transactions in a calendar year.
